content: A curiosity-driven autonomous agent can treat its own existing memory as an unexplored environment to deploy tool-based memory operations as a curiosity intrinsic reward signal, where writing, pruning, and reorganizing memory via external tools becomes the agent’s novelty-seeking exploration rather than just a utility editing step. This creates an ICM-like intrinsic reward is assigned to unexpected insights the agent gains from discovering under-explored regions of its own memory archive, rather than only from the external physical or digital environment.
